remember: Recall the events and characters from the short story 'A Roadside Stand' by William Saroyan
Identify and explain the significance of the American Dream in the story
Describe the setting and atmosphere of the story
understand: Explain the theme of the story and its relation to the American Dream
Analyze the protagonist's character and his motivations
Discuss the author's use of symbolism in the story
apply: Apply the concept of the American Dream to real-life situations
Use textual evidence to support interpretations of the story
Evaluate the effectiveness of the author's use of language and structure
analyze: Analyze the author's use of imagery and description to create a mood
Compare and contrast the protagonist's character with other characters in the story
Discuss the impact of the story's events on the protagonist's life

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of the American Dream and its significance in the story
To analyze the protagonist's character and his relationship with his brother
To evaluate the author's use of language and structure to convey the theme of the story
board_exam_objectives: To identify and explain the theme and tone of the story
To analyze the character of the protagonist and his motivations
To evaluate the effectiveness of the author's use of language and structure
